reasons: AC1 proven: order-entry screenshot shows 'Available to trade: 29.67 USDC' and enabled 'Open long ETH' CTA while availableBalance=0 — exactly the scenario the PR claims to fix.
AC3 proven: withdraw screen shows 'Available balance: $0.00' on the same account, disabled Withdraw button — confirms withdraw still reads availableBalance-only.
AC2 untestable but validated by diff review — no new setInterval/polling hook added, new availableToTradeBalance flows through existing usePerpsLiveAccount channel.
AC4 untestable live (only HL provider present in slot) — covered by PR's new unit test asserting fallback to availableBalance when availableToTradeBalance is absent.
Recipe uses 'call' for navigate-perps-tab and navigate-to-market-detail rather than reinventing them.
Zero forbidden patterns (no switch-default around assertion, no skip-reason eval_sync, no long wait, all node IDs ac<N>/setup/teardown/done).
better_version_guidance: Provider fallback (AC4) could be exercised live by toggling to the aggregated/myx provider mode via switch_provider if a non-HL fixture becomes available in a future slot.
AC2's no-polling claim could be hardened with a cdp_probe that watches background-page network activity for a window and asserts no /spotClearinghouseState polls originate from the extension bundle.
